title: Coprosma reptans
taxonomy: Coprosma reptans is classified as a species within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. This family includes over 660 genera and more than 13,000 species. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Coprosma reptans is a type of flowering plant that is native to New Zealand. The genus Coprosma is a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in New Zealand and other parts of the Pacific Islands. Within the Rubiaceae family, Coprosma reptans is classified as a member of the tribe Rubieae.
uses: Coprosma reptans, also known as the coastal coprosma, has been used in traditional Maori medicine in New Zealand. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It has been used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The Maori people have also used it to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Coprosma reptans for medicinal purposes.
